photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Facade is occupied and readable from the street, with contemporary storefront alterations layered onto the historic building form.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple fascia and coping with limited historic trim visible.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney clearly visible from the selected frontage view.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Decorative character is primarily expressed through the storefront treatment and surviving upper-storey massing.
photo_observations.storefront_description: Closed street-facing shopfront beneath a seasonal clothing sign.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Storefront and signage have been modernized from the original historic condition.

facade_description: 259 Augusta Ave presents a row-house facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, row typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 1, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through a one-storey commercial addition. Through its construction date and typology, each property is associated with the District’s early residential development from t
